title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Respect, and Product Contract · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for reader outcomes, publisher respect, and product contract and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for inclusive feed-reader product contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Define reader population, decision, task journey, accepted delay, failure harm, and observable learning or research value.
Specify respectful identification, fetch cadence, caching, correction, deletion, source pause, and incident contact behavior.
Separate source discovery, ingestion, storage, scheduling, API, reader state, and presentation with explicit accepted unknowns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Feed Ts Reader Job · changed case · Quiz Feed Ts Reader Job
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 6897 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate reader, publisher, inclusive, privacy, and changed-case product outcomes; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define reader population, decision, task journey, accepted delay, failure harm, and observable learning or research value.
